reasoning: The Lean signature captures the informal statement exactly. In Mathlib, `v ∈ eigenspace S c` is defined so that `v ∈ eigenspace S c ↔ S v = c • v` (via `mem_eigenspace_iff`), which allows v = 0 trivially. Axler's convention excludes 0 as an eigenvector, but this only makes the v = 0 case of the Lean hypothesis vacuous, so `∀ v, ∃ c, v ∈ eigenspace S c` is equivalent to "every nonzero v is an eigenvector of S". The conclusion `∃ c : F, S = c • LinearMap.id` is a literal transcription of "S is a scalar multiple of the identity operator". The ambient setting `[AddCommGroup V] [Field F] [Module F V]` matches "vector space" with no extra finite-dimensionality or characteristic hypotheses added or dropped, which is appropriate since the claim holds over any field without finiteness.
